title: OpenAI plans to acquire Ona to give Codex persistent cloud workspaces
friendlyTitle: OpenAI wants Codex agents to keep working in the cloud
summary: OpenAI announced plans to acquire Ona, a secure cloud execution and orchestration company, to expand Codex with persistent environments for long-running AI agents. OpenAI says Codex is now used by more than 5 million people weekly and is increasingly expected to handle work that spans hours or days.
friendlySummary: OpenAI plans to buy Ona, a company that helps run secure cloud workspaces. The goal is to let Codex agents keep working on longer tasks even when the user is not sitting at their computer.
whyItMatters: Persistent, customer-controlled workspaces are a key missing piece for useful agents: they let AI continue tasks after a laptop closes while keeping access, credentials, logs, and review inside enterprise boundaries.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This matters because useful agents need somewhere safe to work. For teams, it could make AI help with bigger projects without giving up control of data and approvals.
